text:
There's a girl named Violet who loved collecting butterflies. One sunny morning when she had nothing better to do, she sat outside on the back porch of her cozy cottage and began reading a book about the life cycles of butterflies.

The sun shone bright and warm as she opened her book and began to daydream. She envisioned a butterfly starting as a small, fluttery egg, eventually pupating into a graceful moth, flying and enjoying the wind, laying eggs of its own, and then dying, completing its life cycle.

As she closed her book, a peculiar fluttering caught her eye. Her eyes drew towards a tiny, iridescent butterfly perched on the edge of the window. Violet reached out and carefully picked it up.

"To me," she whispered.

With a newfound excitement, she began to keep a journal of her butterfly friend's adventures, marking down time and weather and sketching its vivid wings. Over time, Violet noticed changes — the butterfly grew, its colors deepened,
